ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for generic cola drink; SNOMED CT coverage of commercial beverages is limited.

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard single-serve can of cola drink is approximately 330 ml, equivalent to about 330 g, providing roughly 139 kcal and 35 g of carbohydrates.

nutrient_source.title: Cola drink — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Values for energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, calcium, and iron taken directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for cola drink (cr_id D06020007). Sugar is recorded as 0.0 in the HPB source; however, the 10.61 g/100g carbohydrate in a standard cola is understood to derive almost entirely from added sugar — this is a known HPB data-entry convention where total carbohydrate is recorded but sugar sub-fraction is not separately itemised. USDA FoodData Central (FDC ID 174851, Carbonated beverage, cola) was used to supplement saturated fat, potassium, phosphorus, caffeine context, and to cross-validate energy and carbohydrate values.

gi_source.title: Soft drink, cola, regular — Glycemic Index entry
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 63 for regular cola soft drink sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (63 × 35.0) / 100 = 22.1, rounded to 22.0, based on a 330 g serving containing 35.0 g carbohydrate.

hypertension.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with added sugar and phosphoric acid; low potassium; promotes weight gain and insulin resistance which elevate blood pressure
hypertension.guidance: Cola drink is a sugar-sweetened carbonated beverage providing 10.6 g carbohydrate/100 g (entirely from added sugar) with negligible potassium and fibre; regular consumption is associated with elevated blood pressure and should be avoided by individuals managing hypertension — choose plain water, unsweetened sparkling water, or unsweetened tea instead.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (GI 63), rapidly absorbed added sugar (10.6 g/100 g), zero dietary fibre, high glycemic load per standard serving (GL ~22)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Cola drink has a GI of 63 and delivers approximately 35 g of rapidly absorbed sugar per 330 ml can (GL ~22), causing significant postprandial blood glucose spikes; individuals with or at risk of Type 2 Diabetes should avoid regular cola entirely and opt for water or diet/zero-sugar alternatives if carbonated beverages are desired.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High added sugar intake linked to dyslipidaemia, visceral adiposity, and elevated triglycerides; phosphoric acid may adversely affect vascular calcification; no cardioprotective nutrients present
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Regular consumption of cola drinks is associated with increased cardiovascular risk through elevated triglycerides, weight gain, and insulin resistance; individuals with cardiovascular disease should eliminate sugar-sweetened beverages including cola from their diet and replace with water or unsweetened beverages.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Added sugar promotes pro-inflammatory cytokines; phosphoric acid may impair calcium absorption relevant to RA bone health; no anti-inflammatory nutrients present
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Cola drink contains 10.6 g/100 g of added sugar which promotes systemic inflammation and pro-inflammatory mediator production; phosphoric acid may reduce calcium absorption important for RA patients on corticosteroids — avoid cola and choose anti-inflammatory beverages such as green tea or plain water.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Cola drink is a globally popular ultra-processed carbonated soft drink characterised by its distinctive dark caramel colour, sweet flavour, and effervescent texture. It is manufactured by carbonating water and blending it with sugar (or high-fructose corn syrup), caramel colour (Class IV), phosphoric acid, natural cola flavourings, and caffeine. The result is a sweet, slightly acidic, fizzy beverage with a refreshing mouthfeel and a mild bitter finish from caffeine and phosphoric acid. Per 100 g, cola drink provides approximately 42 kcal, 10.6 g of carbohydrate (entirely from added sugar), negligible protein, zero fat, zero dietary fibre, and 6 mg sodium. The sugar content recorded in the HPB Singapore database shows 0.0 g for sugar as a sub-fraction, which is a known data-entry convention; the full 10.6 g carbohydrate is understood to be from added sugar in a standard cola formulation. From a clinical nutrition perspective, cola is one of the highest-risk sugar-sweetened beverages: it has a glycemic index of approximately 63 (medium-high), delivers a glycemic load of approximately 22 per standard 330 ml can, and provides no fibre, protein, vitamins, or minerals to moderate glucose absorption. It is contraindicated for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, and rheumatoid arthritis. Cola is widely consumed globally as a standalone beverage, with meals, or as a mixer in alcoholic drinks. Diet and zero-sugar cola variants exist and are preferable for individuals requiring carbohydrate restriction.
common_user_queries: How much sugar is in cola?
Is cola bad for blood pressure?
How many calories in a can of cola?
Is cola drink suitable for hypertension?
What is the glycemic index of cola?
Can I drink cola if I have heart disease?
What are the health effects of drinking cola regularly?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id D06020007, confidence 0.95 per source metadata). USDA FoodData Central (FDC ID 174851) used to cross-validate and supplement secondary nutrients. GI value sourced from University of Sydney GI Database. All primary sources are approved under Project Veritas v2 source policy.
notes: protein_g is naturally zero — cola drink is a carbohydrate-only beverage containing no protein by formulation. fat_g is naturally zero — cola drink contains no fat by formulation; confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo and USDA FoodData Central (FDC ID 174851). saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, and cholesterol_mg are all confirmed zero as cola contains no fat fraction. sugar_g is recorded as 0.0 in the HPB source data; however, this is a known HPB data-entry convention where total carbohydrate is recorded but the sugar sub-fraction is not separately itemised. The full 10.61 g/100g carbohydrate in a standard cola formulation is derived entirely from added sugar (sucrose or HFCS); sugar_g has therefore been set equal to carbohydrate_g (10.61 g) in this record to reflect nutritional reality, consistent with USDA FoodData Central data for cola. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero — cola contains no fibre. magnesium_mg and selenium_mcg set to -1: these micronutrients are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for cola drink and are below reliable detection thresholds in USDA FoodData Central for this beverage category. potassium_mg value of 2.0 mg/100g and phosphorus_mg value of 11.0 mg/100g are sourced from USDA FoodData Central (FDC ID 174851) as these fields were not reported in the HPB source. water_g of 89.0 g/100g is consistent with USDA FoodData Central data for regular cola. This food is classified as ultra-processed (NOVA Group 4). No cuisine-specific multilingual names are required as cola drink is a globally generic commercial beverage with no specific regional cuisine origin requiring mandatory local-language naming under Project Veritas v2 multilingual rules.
